name: Stuttgart (Baden-Wuerttemberg, Germany)

colors.symbolism: the gold of the city's medieval livery
from the city's coat of arms (a black horse)

description: A horizontal bicolour of yellow over black, charged with the city's coat of arms (a rearing black horse on yellow) at the centre.
history: Stuttgart's yellow-and-black colours derive from the city's medieval coat of arms, which depicts a black rearing stallion (referencing the city's name, from Stuotgarten or 'stud farm'). The flag has been used by the city since the 15th century.

features.symbolism: the historic civic colours of Stuttgart, derived from its coat of arms

features.role: Coat of arms of Stuttgart (a rearing black horse on yellow)
features.symbolism: the rearing stallion alludes to the city's origin as a medieval stud farm (Stuotgarten)
